refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into monitoring, control, and optimization clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved and verified.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and confirming it meets ≤ E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy, control actions, and optimization effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and workflows to improve energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and tonnage sensors with telemetry data aggregation.
DP2: Implement vehicle control algorithms to adjust power usage dynamically.
DP3: Use data analytics and machine learning to optimize operational parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: DP2 depends on DP1 data accuracy.
DP3 depends on data from DP1 and control feedback from DP2.
coupling_check.notes: The DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear data interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Risk of control conflicts if optimization and control algorithms are not well integrated.
High safety criticality requires fail-safe control mechanisms.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface definitions and safety validation between control and optimization DPs.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system integration including energy monitoring, control logic, and optimization analytics.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time energy and tonnage data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands from NGHE Solution to Vehicle for control adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety alerts from Safety Services to adjust energy control strategies.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone for data and control signals among modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes energy efficiency logic and data processing; minimal diffusion.
Contains sensors and actuators but limited processing; moderate diffusion due to control commands.
Primarily for remote control; low containment of energy efficiency functions.
Provides safety alerts impacting energy control but not core energy functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency functions are best contained within the NGHE Solution module to centralize logic and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential performance bottleneck if NGHE Solution becomes too complex.
Dependency on reliable network link for real-time data exchange.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider partitioning optimization analytics into a submodule if complexity grows; ensure robust network redundancy.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly maps to selected DPs with logical decomposition and verifiable sub-FRs.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk between Vehicle and NGHE Solution due to control commands and telemetry data.
Safety Services interface may introduce complexity in control adjustments.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as primary owner; interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and control logs within NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy efficiency functions to NGHE Solution with supporting roles for Vehicle and Safety Services.

recommended_revisions: Define detailed interface protocols for Telemetry and Tasking to reduce diffusion risks.
Plan for safety validation of control algorithms in NGHE Solution.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirements with clear mappings and manageable risks; ready to proceed to implementation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and workflows to improve energy efficiency.

revise.why: No immediate revisions needed; recommended interface and safety validations can be addressed in next design phases.

assumptions: Safety criticality requires robust fail-safe mechanisms.
open_risks: Control and optimization algorithm integration complexity.
Network reliability impacting real-time data exchange.
evidence_needed: Performance data from telemetry and control logs.
Safety validation test results for control algorithms.
